channel.title: CyberNetSec - Regulatory
channel.description: Latest regulatory threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: FinCEN Issues New Guidance to Encourage Banks to Share Fraud-Related Information
item.description:
The U.S. Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) has released updated guidance to encourage financial institutions to voluntarily share information related to suspected fraud. The guidance clarifies that the safe harbor provisions of Section 314(b) of the USA PATRIOT Act apply to th...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: White House Overhauls Cybersecurity Policy for National Security Systems with NSPM-12
item.description:
On June 12, 2026, President Donald J. Trump signed National Security Presidential Memorandum (NSPM-12), a new directive to overhaul the cybersecurity governance for America's most sensitive National Security Systems (NSS). The memo re-establishes the Committee on National Security Systems (CNSS) aft...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canadian Regulator Warns of Risks from Advanced AI in Cybersecurity
item.description:
The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) has issued a cybersecurity alert to its members regarding the dual-use nature of frontier Artificial Intelligence (AI) models. The bulletin, published on June 11, 2026, warns that while advanced AI can enhance cyber defenses, it also significant...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: DHS Cyber Modernization Efforts Face Hurdles, GAO Report Finds
item.description:
A new report from the U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO) finds that while major cybersecurity programs at the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) are strengthening federal cyber defenses, they face significant hurdles. The June 12, 2026 report highlights challenges related to mounting cos...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Novo Nordisk Confirms Data Breach, Urges Vigilance from Trial Participants
item.description:
Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk announced on June 11, 2026, that it suffered a cybersecurity incident where attackers gained unauthorized access to internal IT systems. The company confirmed that personal data related to some clinical trial participants was accessed. While Novo Nordisk st...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Risk-Based Patching for Federal Agencies with New Directive BOD 26-04
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04, fundamentally changing how federal civilian agencies must manage cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The new directive, effective June 10, 2026, mandates a risk-based prioritization mode...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: South Korea Hits E-Commerce Giant Coupang with Record $409M Fine Over Data Breach
item.description:
South Korea's data privacy regulator, the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), has levied a historic fine of 624.68 billion won (approx. $409 million) against e-commerce giant Coupang. The penalty stems from a data breach affecting 37.55 million people, which the regulator attributed t...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Patch for Actively Exploited SolarWinds DoS Flaw Added to KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a critical denial-of-service (DoS) vulnerability in SolarWinds Serv-U file transfer software, CVE-2026-28318, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. This action confirms the vulnerability is under active attack...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Global Compliance Reshaped as NIS2, SEC Disclosure Rules, and AI Laws Come into Force
item.description:
A significant wave of new cybersecurity regulations has come into force globally, fundamentally altering the compliance landscape for businesses. Key among them are the European Union's NIS2 Directive, which expands security mandates for critical infrastructure, and the U.S. SEC's new rules requirin...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Hong Kong Regulator Sounds Alarm on AI-Powered Cyberattacks, Mandates Stronger Defenses
item.description:
Hong Kong's financial watchdog, the Securities and Futures Commission (SFC), has issued a stern warning about the escalating threat of AI-powered cyberattacks. The regulator observes that AI is lowering the barrier to entry for cybercrime, enabling more sophisticated phishing and social engineering ...

Severity: High

Categories: Regulatory, Threat Intelligence, Phishing

Read Full Article →
